articles.summary: 500M+ people use Reddit every month. By showing up where these users research and validate products, you can tap into the conversations that drive more trust and higher conversions.

articles.title: HOW GOOGLE'S UNIVERSAL COMMERCE PROTOCOL CHANGES ECOMMERCE SEO
articles.summary: Product feeds and structured data now determine visibility in Google's AI shopping. AI agents can discover, compare, and complete purchases, so optimization targets being chosen, not ranked. Brands compete in the recommendation layer and need accurate product data. Features like Business Agent, Direct Offers, and in-AI checkout integrate brands into transactions. Linking Search Console to Merchant Center helps teams monitor performance and fix issues. Clear product information lets AI match shopper needs to the right items.

articles.title: HOW TO THINK ABOUT INFLUENCERS OR: DEATH TO AUTHENTICITY
articles.summary: Influencer marketing is considered the fourth age of media, after print, TV, and search. Social platforms now let creators shape culture in ways traditional media cannot. Long-term ROI from influencer campaigns is 51% higher than the all-channel average. Brands see a 38% lift in ROI when working with creators over time rather than in one-off deals. Yet 45% of marketers still judge influencers by follower count, and 80% of brands take “baby steps” in creator strategy.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD AN AI SEO STRATEGY THAT OUTLASTS TACTICS
articles.summary: Teams often chase visibility in AI tools without connecting it to revenue or market share. Strong strategies start with research on where audiences use AI and which queries drive pipeline. Behavior studies show user patterns often differ from assumptions. Content structure matters, as 44% of AI citations come from the first 30% of a page, making front-loaded answers critical. Leaders respond better to scenario planning than traffic forecasts, so model outcomes and review the strategy quarterly to stay aligned with fast-moving AI platforms.

articles.title: PARTIFUL: WHAT WE CAN LEARN FROM THE APP THAT KILLED FACEBOOK EVENTS
articles.summary: Partiful outpaced legacy event platforms by embedding growth into its product. Every invite created built-in distribution and FOMO. Serialized content on Substack and TikTok engaged users. Org Profiles and Group Order fostered communities and added utility, while playful responses to competitors reinforced confidence. By aligning product and positioning with Gen Z's desire for low-friction social experiences, Partiful built a self-sustaining growth loop.

articles.title: PRE-TEST CALCULATOR
articles.summary: This calculator is designed to take the guesswork out of choosing parameters for accurate A/B tests. Enter the weekly traffic and conversions of the page being tested and the calculator will tell you how long to run the test.

articles.title: WHAT REAL MARKETERS ARE BUILDING WITH CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: This piece breaks down how marketers are building reusable AI skills in Claude Code and Cowork by turning existing frameworks into structured Markdown files. They connect these skills to data sources like Google Drive, HubSpot, Clay, and Slack. They iterate on live examples until accuracy improves from around 60% to 90%. In some cases, they deploy them to run on schedules via tools like GitHub, Railway, and Vercel. The key is to codify repeat thinking into skills, stack them into workflows, and refine them through real world use rather than prompts alone.

articles.title: THE MINIMUM LOVABLE PRODUCT ERA
articles.summary: Minimal Lovable Products are replacing MVPs because basic functionality is now cheap and easy to copy. AI has cut build costs from about $200K to as little as $20 or free, so simple utility no longer creates an advantage. Most products still sit at the functional and reliable layers, while lovability drives higher retention, LTV, and word of mouth. Emotional touches such as personality, delight moments, and human tone are now the strongest moat since AI can replicate features quickly. Teams should build delight into roadmaps and set a clear standard that every release must feel genuinely enjoyable to use.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN RISK OF A STRONG BRAND HALO
articles.summary: Johnson & Johnson's Tylenol recall built a powerful reputation that later softened scrutiny around repeated legal settlements across its pharmaceutical and consumer businesses. That defining moment created lasting credibility that shaped both public perception and internal culture. When ethical identity becomes central to a brand, misconduct is easier to rationalize. Reputation must be reinforced by ongoing accountability, or it becomes a shield instead of a safeguard.

articles.title: HOW TO INCREASE YOUR "LUCK SURFACE"
articles.summary: Luck surface is how many chances you give luck to find you, and you grow it by doing things in public and sharing your work widely.

articles.title: CAPTURING THE FULL ROI OF REFERRAL PROGRAMS
articles.summary: Customers acquired through referrals generate significantly more downstream value than regular customers.
